title: tristagma hirtellum
intro: Tristagma hirtellum, also known as the Andean iris, is a flowering plant native to the high-altitude regions of South America. The plant's roots and rhizomes have been traditionally used in folk medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Tristagma hirtellum include its ability to treat digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory problems. Tristagma hirtellum can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, although its use should be carefully considered under the guidance of a healthcare professional due to potential interactions with other medications.
